skill.name: code-reviewer
skill.description: Code review guidance for security, performance, maintainability, testing, configuration, and production reliability across common software stacks.
skill.summary: Review code changes for security, quality, performance, and production readiness.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported command execution, C2, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in descriptive Markdown text. No executable scripts, network calls, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the line uses Markdown backticks around a documentation path. It instructs the assistant to open a local resource file only when examples are needed, and contains no shell command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are inline Markdown formatting around resources/implementation-playbook.md. There is no executable code, interpreter call, or user-controlled command string.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Description
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm, but the line is frontmatter prose describing AI-powered code analysis. No cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection appears here.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is descriptive metadata, not code. The likely keyword match is embedded in ordinary words such as description or code.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Tooling Prose
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the line describes real-time feedback integration with CLI tools and IDEs. It does not configure or recommend a cryptographic algorithm.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line is a capability bullet about development tooling. No encryption, hashing, cipher, or security-sensitive code is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Design Pattern Text
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the line references design patterns and architectural consistency. This is a code quality topic, not cryptographic logic.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The trigger appears to be a substring in the word design. The surrounding context is maintainability review guidance.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Feedback Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the line says the skill provides actionable feedback with examples. It does not include cryptographic code or advice.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is behavioral guidance for review output. There is no algorithm name used as a security recommendation or implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Architecture Prose
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the line lists software architecture patterns and design principles. It contains no executable or security-sensitive cryptographic content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The likely match is the substring DES within design. Context confirms this is architecture knowledge, not cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword in Compliance Acronym
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged C2 terminology, but the line references SOC2, PCI DSS, and GDPR compliance requirements. These are legitimate audit and compliance topics for code review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SOC2 contains the substring C2, but the full line is regulatory compliance prose. No command-and-control behavior or network endpoint is described.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Keyword in Section Heading
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged network reconnaissance, but the line is the heading Team Collaboration and Process. It contains no scanning command, host discovery, or network access instruction.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a plain Markdown heading. No reconnaissance tool, network target, or data collection workflow appears at this location.

content.value_statement: Code changes can hide security, performance, and reliability issues before release. This skill guides structured reviews that prioritize actionable findings and safer deployments.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code for security vulnerabilities and unsafe implementation patterns.
Evaluates performance, scalability, and resource management risks.
Assesses tests, maintainability, architecture, and technical debt.
Reviews configuration, CI/CD, infrastructure, and deployment changes.
Organizes feedback by severity with practical remediation guidance.
Suggests static analysis and dependency scanning tools for review workflows.
limitations: It does not execute scans or tests unless the host AI tool provides those capabilities.
It depends on the code, configuration, and context supplied by the user.
It cannot guarantee complete vulnerability coverage for complex systems.
It may need project-specific standards to judge style and architecture accurately.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Identify security, reliability, and maintainability issues before a pull request is merged.

use_cases.title: Production Risk Assessment
use_cases.description: Review migrations, configuration changes, and deployment updates for operational risk.

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Code Audit
use_cases.description: Check authentication, authorization, input validation, secrets handling, and dependency risks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for correctness, security, tests, and maintainability. Prioritize findings by severity and include clear remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Security Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for application security issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for authentication, authorization, input validation, injection risks, secrets handling, and unsafe dependencies. Explain each risk and recommend fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Performance and Reliability Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review of backend or infrastructure changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this change for performance bottlenecks, resource leaks, concurrency issues, failure handling, observability, and production reliability concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Full Release Gate Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a release gate review across code, tests, configuration, dependencies, CI/CD, and rollback readiness. Separate blocking issues from non-blocking improvements.

output_examples.input: Review an API endpoint change before merge.
output_examples.output: A severity-ranked review with findings on validation, authorization, error handling, tests, and concrete remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Assess a database migration for production risk.
output_examples.output: A deployment-focused assessment covering locking, rollback strategy, data integrity, observability, and release sequencing.
A security and reliability review covering secrets use, permissions, quality gates, artifact handling, and failure recovery.

best_practices: Provide the diff, surrounding files, test output, and deployment context for better findings.
Ask for severity-ranked feedback when the review must support merge decisions.
State project standards, threat model, and performance targets before complex reviews.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a broad review without sharing the relevant code or configuration.
Do not treat AI review as a replacement for tests, scanners, or human approval.
Do not ignore low-severity findings that indicate repeated design or testing gaps.

faq.question: Can this skill run static analysis tools?
faq.answer: It can recommend tools and interpret results. Tool execution depends on the host environment and available permissions.

faq.question: Does it focus only on security?
faq.answer: No. It also covers correctness, performance, maintainability, testing, configuration, and production reliability.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide the diff, related files, expected behavior, test results, and any deployment or compliance constraints.

faq.question: Can it review infrastructure changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for CI/CD, Kubernetes, Terraform, containers, secrets, monitoring, and deployment risk.

faq.question: Will it guarantee secure code?
faq.answer: No. It supports review workflows but cannot guarantee complete coverage or replace formal security testing.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
